root_cause_hypothesis: Aggregate spoke traffic exceeding hub uplink capacity during peak windows; no per-spoke shaping.
evidence: Sustained rise in hub-facing link utilization (>85%) with upward jitter trend; precedes loss-based SLA breach on spoke tunnels.
recommended_actions: Apply QoS shaping / LLQ on the hub uplink to protect priority classes.
Temporarily re-home one high-volume spoke to the secondary hub.
Raise hub uplink committed rate or enable burst allowance if contracted.
citation: RB-CONGESTION-01 -- NOC Runbook v3.2, sec. 4.1 (Hub Congestion)
